---
title: ""
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header">
  <h1><i class="bi bi-book-fill"></i> Learning Resources</h1>
  <p>ECO 1000 · Principles of Economics · Fall 2026</p>
</div>
```

## E-Book & Core Readings

*[Add your e-book access link and instructions here.]*

The primary textbook for this course is available online. You can access it through:

- **[E-Book Portal]** — *[Add link]*
- **Elon Library reserves** — *[Add link or instructions]*

---

## Chapter Materials

Each week's readings and supplemental notes will be posted here as the semester progresses.

| Week | Topic | Materials |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Introduction | *[Link to notes/slides]* |
| 2 | Thinking Like an Economist | *[Link to notes/slides]* |
| 3 | Supply & Demand | *[Link to notes/slides]* |

---

## Videos & Outside Resources

A few high-quality free resources to supplement your reading:

- **[Marginal Revolution University](https://mru.org)** — Short, engaging economics videos organized by topic.
- **[Khan Academy Economics](https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain/ap-macroeconomics)** — Good for review and practice.
- **[The Economist](https://www.economist.com)** — Real-world applications of course concepts (Elon library provides access).

---

## Lecture Slides

Slides will be posted after each class session.

::: {.callout-note}
Slides are a supplement to class — not a substitute for attendance. Discussion, examples, and problem-solving happen in real time.
:::

*[Slides will be linked here as the semester progresses.]*
